content:
About me#Introduced to piano and music theory from a very young age, I discovered my passion for music there.
Very quickly, I explored computer-based music production. I already enjoyed composing while learning piano, but the vast creative possibilities offered by DAWs won me over.
Initially, I was headed toward computer engineering studies (I’ve always loved tinkering with computers) but during my studies, I questioned this path. I felt that, on a human level, I needed to contribute meaningfully, to find a field where I could give the best of myself. I then turned to a degree in sound engineering, a program that would allow me to develop a comprehensive approach as a composer.
My learning of music theory has been mostly empirical and self-taught over the years. I first discovered composition techniques, and then, through research, I was able to identify them: cadences, scale degrees, harmonic progressions, etc. In DAW-based production, since composition alone isn’t enough, I did extensive research on mixing, mastering, and sound design, knowledge that my studies later complemented.
Today, I aim to showcase my skills and my creativity.
Inspiration#My musical development has been deeply influenced by artists like Nujabes, LTJ Bukem, Shiro Sagisu, Mandragora, Brian Bennett, and Koji Sugiyama.
Here’s a non-exhaustive selection of music and albums that has shaped and inspired my journey :
The Mystery Kindaichi Band - The Adventure of Kohsuke Kindaichi#Shiro Sagisu - Thanatos#Michel Legrand - L’été 42#I am deeply moved by the emotional power of Michel Legrand’s and Shiro Sagisu’s works.
Pangya OST#Garoad - VA-11 HALL-A#Total Science Volume 2#

Introduction#I’ve worked with several DAWs, and I currently compose with Bitwig Studio. I’m also expanding my skill set by learning Wwise for interactive audio integration.
Below is a selection of my work. If you’d like to explore further, you can check out my YouTube channel here
All music featured here is registered with SACEM, and I retain full rights to these works.
Projects#(not) BGM Compilation (2026)#(not) BGM Compilation is my latest released project. Originally conceived as a portfolio for game music composition, this EP aims to create a cohesive musical universe that mirrors the various moments of a fictional adventure. To achieve this, I used the Roland JV-1080, a synth iconic to the early 2000s gaming aesthetic. I also challenged myself to compose the entire EP in just two weeks, pushing my creativity and experimenting with the pressures of a tight deadline.
Roland SC-88 experiment (2025)#Inspired by the music of LTJ Bukem and Pangya, I developed a fascination with the aesthetic origins of their soundscapes. This led me to experiment with the Roland SC-88 and the Zero-G Datafiles sample discs, seeking to blend Pangya’s lighthearted universe with the raw energy of drum and bass.
Eyes Without a Face (2024)#This project is an original score for a scene from Georges Franju’s Eyes Without a Face that was originally silent. To amplify the film’s unsettling atmosphere, I drew inspiration from Steve Reich’s polyrhythms, creating a disorienting yet hypnotic soundscape, as well as Bernard Herrmann’s signature use of the major seventh interval, a powerful tool for building relentless tension. I also worked within specific artistic and technical constraints: weaving the film’s existing leitmotif into my composition, limiting instrumentation to a small ensemble and the Hammond organ to stay true to the 1960s sound, and carefully crafting the sound design to artificially age the recordings—adding analog warmth and saturation to match the era’s sonic texture. Your browser cannot play this video. Download video.
From 3:32 to 4:44: original film score.Lise Terre and Lise Nuit (2023)#This project is an original soundtrack composed for an initiatory tale intended for children. Music for children is generally composed using organic instruments; it is an opportunity for composers to present the different families of instruments such as brass, string instruments, and woodwinds. Here, my intention was to introduce children to electronic music by using synthesizers with a maximum of two oscillators and simple waveforms: limiting myself to sine, square, and triangle waves, which are the fundamental waveforms of electronic music.
To compose, I drew inspiration from three artists:
First, Claude Larson, who demonstrated that synthesizers can also be vectors of emotion—a notion I hold dear. In the context of music for children, I don’t think it’s very interesting to create irony with melody; the only function this soundtrack needed to fulfill was to accompany the action and highlight the emotion. The philosophy of this music was therefore for children to be able to associate electronic music with feelings.
For the pure composition, I was inspired by Prokofiev. I associated themes and synthesizers with the characters; these themes evolve depending on the action. For example, the themes of Little Brother and Little Sister are linked when they are together and separate when they find themselves apart. Miel, who represents the mentor in Little Brother’s initiation, has a theme close to his, etc.
Finally, I drew inspiration from Steve Reich, who is a pillar in electronic music culture. I mention him several times by using polyrhythms. This technique allows me to maintain tension and connect different scenes.
Insadium (2022)#Insadium is a series of 7 introspective portraits. I composed a soundtrack that accompanies the enigmatic and painful narration of these works. The music evolves with the portraits, alternating between moments of calm and tension, to highlight their emotional contrasts. The goal is to create total immersion, where the viewer is immersed into an experience that is both visual and auditory.
Eclectic Tracks#I love experimenting with different styles. Here is a selection of projects showcasing the diversity of my work.
Pepe la pefra
cathartic Techno Trippin Bossar
ambient Trip-Hop Eva
jazzish love music Walking on side b.
MIDI-composed jazz seeking live musicians for recording. Explorer22
Ambient space inspired track.
title: Portfolio
